mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing equity investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer prices indicate stagflationary demand destruction, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy and suppressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when RSAFS falls and CPIAUCSL rises, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, generating immediate positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when applied to late-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 100 thousand in a single month confirm robust economic expansion and strong corporate revenue growth, prompting institutional investors to increase equity allocations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in over 60% of months where payroll growth exceeds 100 thousand, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over baseline trends.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in housing starts and real GDP growth signal synchronized strength across residential and broad economic demand, reducing recession probabilities and supporting higher equity risk premiums over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP growth signal does not yield statistically significant positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 250 trigger aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and forcing portfolio de-risking that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 during high inflation regimes, or the threshold signal fails to maintain a negative Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures reflect immediate consumer spending momentum that translates directly into corporate earnings revisions, generating short-term positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal predicts negative returns in more than half of the observed periods during stable macro conditions.
